verifier_values.criteria: States that new onset back pain in a patient with a history of breast cancer can indicate spinal metastasis.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
The script (Onc_Patient 9 script2.pdf) includes back pain complaint: "Doctor, I’ve been having some aching back pain for the past month."

"Spinal metastases are common in cancer, but they cause spinal cord compression only when they extend from the bone into the epidural space (Figure 2A). Aching back pain and tenderness on percussion over the affected site are typical and may precede neurologic features by several weeks. Pain may be worse when the patient is supine and causes awakening from sleep." -https://www.nejm.org/doi/full/10.1056/NEJMra1516539?utm_source=openevidence

Biopsy of right breast mass and right lymph node on date of 12/31/2020 showed invasive ductal carcinoma and a positive lymph node.  "ER status: negative; PR status negative, Her2 status positive; Grade 3."
 [CLINICALNOTES], Note type - [H&P], Column - [D], Row - [14]

verifier_values.criteria: States there are sclerotic lesions on the T8 and T10 vertebrae in the thoracic spine X-ray.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: "There are subtle areas of increased sclerosis involving the T8 and T10 vertebral bodies without associated cortical destruction. Pedicles are partially visualized and appear mildly asymmetric at T10. No acute fracture is identified." - attached X-ray (Onc_Patient 9 spine2.pdf)

verifier_values.criteria: States that there is increased FDG uptake in the T8 and T10 vertebrae on the PET scan.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
"Osseous Structures: 
There is mildly increased FDG uptake within the T8 and T10 vertebral bodies, with SUV values 
only slightly above background marrow activity. Corresponding CT images demonstrate subtle 
sclerotic changes at these levels without cortical destruction, expansile lesion, or associated soft 
tissue mass." - attached PET scan (Onc_Patient 9 pet.pdf)

verifier_values.criteria: States that the imaging results are suspicious for bony metastases from breast cancer in the T8 and T10 vertebrae.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
"8F]FDG-PET is an important tool for the diagnosis of early osseous metastatic disease because increased glucose metabolism in neoplastic cells can become evident in even the earliest cases of bone marrow infiltration." - https://www.ajnr.org/content/43/3/332

"For patients presenting with metastatic disease, the appearance of the lesions may help to guide the search for a primary. Some helpful patterns include lytic and sclerotic lesions, "expanded" lesions, "pseudosarcomatous" lesions, acral lesions, and soft tissue ossification." -https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/9362427/

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient likely has stage IV metastatic breast cancer.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: "Stage 4 breast cancer is advanced breast cancer that has metastasized, or spread, from the original location in the breast to other organs of the body, such as the bones, lungs, liver, or brain. Stage 4 breast cancer is also called metastatic breast cancer or breast cancer recurrence." -https://www.nationalbreastcancer.org/breast-cancer-stage-4/

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient's current age is 60 (acceptable range 59 to 61).
